xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Run enrichment analysis using information theory

Source: R/run_info_enrichment.R
run_info_enrichment.Rd

Scores gene sets by mutual information (MI) between gene list membership and pathway membership, rather than by raw overlap count alone. This addresses a known limitation of classical over-representation analysis (ORA): large gene sets can achieve highly significant hypergeometric p-values through sheer size even when the overlap is biologically unspecific. MI naturally penalises uninformative sets because when a pathway covers a large fraction of the universe, membership conveys little information and info_bits approaches zero.

run_info_enrichment(
  gene_list,
  gene_sets,
  universe = NULL,
  n_perm = 1000,
  min_size = 5,
  max_size = Inf,
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

gene_list

Character vector of query gene symbols.

gene_sets

Named list of character vectors defining gene set membership (e.g. GO terms, pathways).

universe

Character vector of background genes. If NULL (default), the union of all genes across gene_sets is used and a message is emitted. Supplying an explicit universe is strongly recommended for reproducibility.

n_perm

Integer. Number of hypergeometric draws used to build the permutation null for emp_p and z_score. Default 1000.

min_size

Integer. Gene sets with fewer than min_size members after intersection with universe are silently dropped. Default 5.

max_size

Integer. Gene sets with more than max_size members after intersection with universe are silently dropped. Defaults to Inf (no upper limit). Note that MI naturally down-weights large, uninformative sets, so filtering is a computational convenience rather than a mathematical necessity.

seed

Optional integer passed to set.seed() for reproducible permutations. NULL (default) leaves the RNG state untouched.

Value

A data frame with one row per gene set (after size filtering), containing:

set

Gene set name.

overlap

Number of query genes in the set (k).

set_size

Gene set size after universe intersection (m).

list_size

Query list size after universe intersection (n).

universe_size

Background size (N).

expected

Expected overlap under the null (n * m / N).

ratio

Observed / expected overlap ratio.

info_bits

Mutual information I(list ; set) in bits.

signed_bits

Signed MI: positive when overlap > expected (enrichment), negative when below (depletion). The sign is derived from the direction of overlap, not from MI itself, which is always non-negative.

z_score

Standard scores of info_bits relative to the permutation null. NA when null standard deviation is zero or n_perm is too small to estimate it reliably.

emp_p

Empirical p-value: proportion of null bits >= observed, with a +1 / (n_perm + 1) continuity correction.

p_enrich

Exact hypergeometric enrichment p-value.

p_deplete

Exact hypergeometric depletion p-value.

null_mean, null_sd

Mean and SD of the permutation null bits distribution.

padj_enrich, padj_deplete, padj_emp

BH-adjusted versions of the corresponding p-values.

Details

Two complementary p-values are returned:

p_enrich

Exact one-sided hypergeometric p-value on the overlap count k. Comparable to standard ORA tools.

emp_p

Empirical p-value derived from a permutation null on information bits. Ranks pathways by how surprising their MI is, not just their overlap size.

When the two measures disagree, the disagreement is itself informative: a set with low p_enrich but high emp_p is large and overlaps by expectation; a set with high p_enrich but low emp_p is small yet unusually precise. Users may sort by either depending on their question.

See also

run_enrichment() for classical ORA/GSEA.

Examples

universe <- paste0("G", 1:500)
gene_list <- sample(universe, 50)
gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A = sample(universe, 30),
  pathway_B = sample(universe, 100),
  pathway_C = sample(universe, 10)
)

res <- run_info_enrichment(
  gene_list = gene_list,
  gene_sets = gene_sets,
  universe  = universe,
  n_perm    = 500,
  seed      = 42
)
head(res[order(res$emp_p), ])
#>         set overlap set_size list_size universe_size expected    ratio
#> 1 pathway_A       5       30        50           500        3 1.666667
#> 2 pathway_B      10      100        50           500       10 1.000000
#> 3 pathway_C       1       10        50           500        1 1.000000
#>      info_bits  signed_bits    z_score     emp_p  p_enrich p_deplete
#> 1 0.0005135785 0.0005135785 -0.2031876 0.3772455 0.1695801 0.9329547
#> 2 0.0000000000 0.0000000000 -0.4069002 1.0000000 0.5622195 0.5851484
#> 3 0.0000000000 0.0000000000 -0.7800788 1.0000000 0.6548378 0.7365025
#>      null_mean     null_sd padj_enrich padj_deplete padj_emp
#> 1 0.0009242809 0.002021296   0.5087403    0.9329547        1
#> 2 0.0007463602 0.001834259   0.6548378    0.9329547        1
#> 3 0.0013726539 0.001759635   0.6548378    0.9329547        1
